data.body:
## What happened

During commit workflow for bug 62d3-7064, the review resolution loop escalated from standard tier (sonnet) to deep-arch tier (opus) on pass 3, but dispatched dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch DIRECTLY — without first running the three parallel sonnet sub-agents (dso:code-reviewer-deep-correctness, dso:code-reviewer-deep-verification, dso:code-reviewer-deep-hygiene).

The deep tier is a unified system: 3 parallel sonnet agents produce specialist findings, then the opus arch agent synthesizes them. Dispatching arch without the sonnet findings violates the single-writer invariant and produces a review based on opus reading the diff alone rather than synthesizing specialist analysis.

## Root cause

REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md's re-review model escalation table (line ~473) maps pass 3+ to dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch, but this escalation path bypasses the deep-tier dispatch sequence (Step 4 Deep Tier section). The escalation should either:
1. Escalate to the full deep tier (3 sonnet + opus synthesis), or
2. Escalate to standard-tier opus (dso:code-reviewer-standard with model override to opus) which is a single agent that can review independently

## Fix needed

1. dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch agent definition should reject invocations that don't include SONNET_A/B/C findings in the prompt — fail-fast rather than silently producing a non-synthesis review
2. REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md re-review escalation should route pass 3+ to dso:code-reviewer-standard with model=opus (not deep-arch) when the full deep-tier dispatch sequence is not being followed
